subject: Public Health
title: Endemic, Epidemic, and Pandemic
description: A quick, practical guide to Endemic, Epidemic, and Pandemic.

hook.text: Malaria is endemic. Ebola was an epidemic. COVID-19 became a pandemic. The words matter—and they mean different things.

buildup.text: Endemic means a disease persists at a constant level. Epidemic means cases suddenly surge beyond the baseline.

discovery.text: A pandemic is an epidemic that crosses international boundaries—affecting multiple countries or continents.

twist.text: The WHO doesn't have a formal threshold for declaring a pandemic—it's partly a judgment call based on spread.

climax.text: Understanding these categories guides the scale and type of public health response—local versus global mobilization.

punchline.text: Endemic: always there. Epidemic: surging. Pandemic: nowhere is safe.

quiz.question: What distinguishes a pandemic from an epidemic?
options: A pandemic only affects one country
